stance: Risk-on within Healthcare: overweight names where strong price momentum AND rising analyst revisions agree; cut late-momentum names where revisions are falling.
experience_note: last_round_review is null — early round, nothing graded yet, so these theses are pre-registered cleanly for honest scoring. What I am watching: (1) whether the momentum+revisions-agree cohort (Acutaas, Gland, Rubicon, Granules, Laurus, Natco, Zydus, Marksans) actually outperforms my benchmark over the next 1-3 months — if it does not, the momentum-led brain is mis-specified for pharma right now. (2) Whether the names I CUT for falling revisions (Shilpa, Emcure, Biocon, Piramal Pharma) keep rallying anyway — if the late-momentum names I dropped beat the names I added, my read that falling revisions kill momentum is wrong and I should weight price momentum more, revisions less. (3) Whether my fade of Blue Jet (overweighted by the quant on a 7.86 valuation z with negative momentum) is vindicated by it lagging. What would change my mind: a clear pattern where falling-revision momentum names continue to win — that would tell me revisions are a weak/lagging force in this universe and I should defer more to the quant baseline.
